vBRIEFInfo.author: agent:claude-code
vBRIEFInfo.description: PAN-1030: Surface awaiting user input state on kanban, command deck, and inspector

plan.title: Surface awaiting user input state on kanban + command deck
plan.author: agent:claude-code

narratives.Problem: Dashboard users need a clear, glanceable indication when work or planning agents are blocked on user input such as Claude Code permission prompts, planning Done prompts, Pi harness prompts, or generic y/n confirmations.
narratives.Approach: Detect awaiting-input state observationally from existing runtime waiting signals, AskUserQuestion JSONL entries, and read-only tmux pane scans; then flow the state through agent enrichment, contracts, the read model, and existing dashboard surfaces without mutating agent state.

items.title: Restore awaiting-input detection and indicators across dashboard surfaces

narrative.Action: Implement read-only awaiting-input detection, expose it through agent enrichment and shared contract types, and render INPUT-style indicators with prompt context in kanban cards, Command Deck issue rows, and the issue inspector.

subItems.title: Kanban issue cards show an awaiting-input badge for blocked primary agents or planning sessions
Command Deck issue rows show the same badge in the agent column with explanatory prompt text
Issue Inspector surfaces exact prompt text and terminal attach path for answering in one click
Awaiting-input badge clears automatically after the agent moves past the prompt
Detection covers Claude Code permission prompts, planning Done prompts, Pi prompts, and generic y/n confirmations
Detection is read-only and uses capture-pane heuristics without agent state mutation
Updates flow through the existing dashboard read-model heartbeat and event stream
